nameMeanings.name: Blair
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from any of numerous places in Scotland called Blair, named with Scottish Gaelic blàr (genitive blàir) ‘plain, field’, especially a battlefield (Irish blár). There were families of this name in the Middle Ages taking their names from any of the places called Blair in Dysart parish Fife, Dalry parish Ayrshire, and Blairgowrie in Perthshire.
Americanized form of French   Belair   and   Blais  .

variants.name: Belair
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Lucien, Aime, Auguste, Aurele, Carmelle, Normand, Raoul, Rolande.

French (also Bélair): habitational name from Belair, Bélair or (Le) Bel-Air, names of several places in various parts of France, meaning literally ‘beautiful place’. Compare Belaire , Bellair , Bellaire , and Blair .

nameMeanings.name: Ann
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant spelling of Anne . In the U.S. Ann is the more frequent spelling.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Ann Margaret Blair was born in 1610, in Ayrshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, her father, Gavin Blair, was 34 and her mother, Isabel Crawford, was 25. She had at least 1 son with Andrew Lynn. She died in 1660, in her hometown, at the age of 50.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ann Margaret Blair est née en 1610, à Ayrshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, Gavin Blair, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Isabel Crawford, avait 25 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Andrew Lynn. Elle est décédée en 1660, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 50 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Ann Margaret Blair 1610 in Ayrshire, Schottland, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Gavin Blair 34 und ihre Mutter Isabel Crawford 25. Sie hatte mit Andrew Lynn mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1660 im Alter von 50 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Ann Margaret Blair è nata il nel 1610 nel Ayrshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, sua padre, Gavin Blair, aveva 34 anni e sua madre, Isabel Crawford, aveva 25 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con Andrew Lynn. Morì nel 1660, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 50 anni.
